theme: deep-water berth admission under a drifting tariff regime
title: Tidewater Berth: Admission Under Value Drift
strategies: open-door: bar 0 everywhere, admit any vessel that still fits in arrival order -- reproduces the evaluator's weak anchor (~0.1)

early-fitted per-class bar: admit any vessel whose fee beats its class's published early-regime mean, flat across berth-fullness and drift -- the obvious move, spends credits on cheap early water and misses the late deep-sea whales on drifting tides

shadow-price reservation: read the full class schedule, reserve credits for the scarce deep-sea demand, gate coastal/regional admission on how full the berth is (rem_bucket) so the last credits are held for whales; release the bar once the drift probe (sig_bucket) detects the tariff regime shifting up

richer bid-price / drift-detection policies: shape the bar surface across (class, rem_bucket, sig_bucket) more finely -- e.g. earlier drift detection, per-class shadow prices, tuned reserve lines -- to push admitted value above the reserving baseline toward the offline hindsight optimum
